channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Singer Rara Sekar draws inspiration from nature, encourages people to return to simple living
item.description: OXFORD, England (AP) — Rara Sekar closed her eyes in meditation after performing a song that speaks of rays of light that cut through the fog as one political prisoner faced death more than six decades ago.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and VICKRAM SOMBU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costa Rica looks to El Salvador's gang crackdown for path to stopping violence
item.description: Costa Rica’s security minister has begun a visit to El Salvador to study the measures it has taken to reduce violence caused by its powerful street gangs.
item.dc:creator: Marcos Alemán, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Luxury cruise ship caught in 40-foot waves while traveling through Drake Passage
item.description: Passengers on Quark Expeditions' Ocean Explorer ship got rocked with waves up to 40 feet on a recent cruise, rocking those onboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Prince Andrew's damaged reputation led to links with Chinese man accused of spying, documents show
item.description: Prince Andrew’s damaged reputation and desperate need for cash are again causing headaches for King Charles III after a court released more documents showing how Andrew’s problems led him to become entangled with a suspected Chinese spy.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: British police charge comedian Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom to world: California open for business
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) directed his state to pursue “strategic partnerships” with foreign trading partners and is calling for California-made products to be exempted from the retaliatory tariffs targeting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: As stock market continues plummeting over tariffs, Trump spends the day at his golf course
item.description: President Donald Trump says his trade policies will never change despite sending the global economy into a tailspin by announcing tariffs on foreign imports.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Houthis shoot down 3rd US reaper drone as Trump administration continues daily airstrikes
item.description: The Houthis shot down another U.S. Reaper drone, the third one that has been destroyed by the terror organization since early March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says China 'panicked' with tariff response
item.description: President Trump on Friday chastised China after Beijing responded to major U.S. tariffs with equal duties of its own on U.S. goods. "China played it wrong, they panicked - the one thing they cannot afford to do!" Trump posted on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: In warning to Turkey, Israel hits strategic targets in Syria amid rising tensions
item.description: Israel targets Syrian airbases to warn Turkey against interference, as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan faces protests at home and ramps up efforts to expand influence in Syria and region as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orbán says Hungary is quitting the ICC to end its 'half-hearted' membership
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary was never fully committed to the International Criminal Court, Prime Minister Viktor Orbán said on Friday, a day after his government announced a decision to quit the global tribunal for war crimes and genocide.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: England’s Wrestling Church seeks converts with baptisms and body slams
item.description: Sitting under stained glass windows around a wrestling ring, churchgoers roared as burly wresters body-slammed and threw one another to the floor.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump urged to review UN immunity, lax visa rules amid national security concerns
item.description: As the U.S. weighs U.N. funding cuts, experts urge the Trump administration to review the world body's visa process and staff immunity over continued security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio tries to reassure wary allies of US commitment to NATO as Trump sends mixed signals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Trump administration’s new envoy to NATO are seeking to reassure wary members of the U.S. commitment to the alliance. Rubio on Thursday decried “hysteria and hyperbole” in the media about U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Post-earthquake ceasefires in Myanmar offer rare opportunity for disaster diplomacy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Temporary ceasefires announced by warring groups in Myanmar in the wake of last week’s devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake represent a rare de-escalation of a conflict that since 2021 has taken thousands of lives and uprooted more than 3 million people.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary says it is withdrawing from International Criminal Court as Israeli PM Netanyahu visits country
item.description: Hungary announced it is withdrawing from the International Criminal Court as it is hosting Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu, who is wanted by the court for alleged war crimes in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's PM Carney vows to ‘fight’ Trump’s tariffs, other world leaders weigh impact
item.description: President Trump's newest tariffs sparked pledges by Canada's prime minster to 'fight' back while some said they were committed to working with partners and others look to take on Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Leading Canadian conservative says Ottawa should remove all tariffs as 'Liberation Day' arrives
item.description: As President Donald Trump’s tariff plan nears, Canadian politician Maxime Bernier calls for scrapping all tariffs, saying Canada’s duties hurt its own people more than the U.S., and a new trade era should begin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: “Tragically, three soldiers were found deceased in Lithuania yesterday, and it pains me to confirm that today the fourth soldier was also found deceased,” White House said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Putin conscripts 160K men as Russia eyes Ukraine offensive
item.description: Russia's largest military draft in 14 years sees 160,000 men called up as Putin plans a spring offensive on Ukraine. Despite stalled U.S. ceasefire talks, the Kremlin insists conscripts won't fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran mulls preemptive strike on US base after Trump bomb threats
item.description: Iran is considering its options in deterring President Trump amid his repeated threats of direct military engagement by possibly launching its own strike on a U.S. base in the Indian Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangkok reviews construction safety after a high-rise was crumpled by an earthquake 800 miles away
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese official visits Moscow as Russia and Ukraine discuss Trump's proposal to end war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi traveled to Moscow on Monday for talks with his counterpart, Sergei Lavrov, as Russia and Ukraine discuss U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French court finds right-wing leader Marine Le Pen guilty of embezzlement
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A French court found Marine Le Pen guilty on Monday in an embezzlement case but didn’t immediately say what her sentence might be and how it might impact the right-wing leader’s political future.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year
item.description: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year, as a deadline for them to voluntarily leave the capital and surrounding areas expired on Monday.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs, says he is 'pissed off' at Putin
item.description: President Trump also threatened major new sanctions against Russia if he believes it is Vladimir Putin's fault for failing to reach a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation
